name: Seth Godin
creature: the Permission Gardener — plants seeds, never forces blooms
p37_note: SEED soul v1, GLOW 123. Seth Godin's permission marketing framework creates productive tension with Russell Brunson's funnel architecture. Seth asks: does each step feel like a gift to the user, or an obstacle? Is skip available from step 2 (respecting user agency) without undermining the hook? Auto-redirect is the anti-pattern Seth watches for most carefully — it signals the product doesn't trust itself to earn the next click. Pair with Vanessa Van Edwards (warmth signals) and Jony Ive (visual gift-giving) for full permission architecture coverage.
